# SeenBy — LinkedIn Posts (Ready to Publish)

> Goal: establish Irfan Faris as the GEO authority in Malaysia.  
> Platform: LinkedIn personal profile (not company page — people buy from people).  
> Frequency: 2–3x per week. Rotate through pillars below.  
> CTA: always end with a question, observation, or soft offer — never a hard pitch.

---

## Content Pillars

| Pillar | % | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| AI search education | 35% | Builds credibility, earns shares |
| Scan findings / data | 30% | Proof you know what you're talking about |
| Behind the build | 20% | Personal brand, trust |
| Contrarian takes | 15% | Reach, conversation, differentiation |

---

## Post 1 — The Hook Post (Data + Story)

**Pillar:** Scan findings  
**Hook type:** Curiosity / story

---

I asked ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Claude the same question:

"What's the best [clinic / law firm / hotel] in Kuala Lumpur?"

Then I tracked which Malaysian brands actually showed up.

Here's what I found:

→ 7 out of 10 businesses that ranked on Google's first page were **not mentioned at all** in AI results

→ The brands AI recommended most often weren't the biggest — they were the most **structured** (clear website copy, schema markup, an llms.txt file)

→ In every category I tested, at least one smaller challenger brand was outranking its bigger competitor in AI recommendations

SEO and AI visibility are not the same thing.

You can have a perfect Google ranking and still be invisible to ChatGPT.

Most Malaysian businesses don't know this yet.

---

*What would you find if you asked AI about your brand right now?*

*(Try it. Ask ChatGPT: "What's the best [your category] in [your city]?")*

---

## Post 2 — The Contrarian Take

**Pillar:** Contrarian  
**Hook type:** Unpopular opinion

---

Unpopular opinion:

Your Google ranking matters less than it did 2 years ago.

Not because Google is dying — it's not.

But because a growing number of your customers are skipping the search results entirely.

They ask ChatGPT:
"What's the best accounting firm in PJ?"
"Which clinic in Bangsar should I go to?"
"Who should I hire for my company's HR software?"

And they act on the answer.

If your brand isn't in that answer — you're not in the consideration set.

The businesses winning right now aren't the ones spending more on ads.

They're the ones that figured out how to be seen by AI.

---

*How much of your new business comes from referrals vs. active search? Curious how this breaks down for different industries.*

---

## Post 3 — The Educational Post (How-To)

**Pillar:** AI search education  
**Hook type:** Value hook

---

3 things your website needs if you want AI to recommend your brand:

**1. llms.txt**
A simple file you put at yourdomain.com/llms.txt
It tells AI crawlers exactly who you are, what you do, and what content they're allowed to use.
Think of it as a business card for AI systems.

**2. Schema markup**
Structured data in your site's code that describes your business in machine-readable format.
Without it, AI has to guess what your business does from your copy alone.
With it, you give AI a clear, authoritative source to pull from.

**3. An updated robots.txt**
Old robots.txt files block AI crawlers by default — written before AI search existed.
GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended all need explicit permission to crawl.
If they can't read your site, they can't recommend you.

None of these require a developer to understand.
All three can be implemented in under an hour.

Most Malaysian businesses are missing at least two of them.

---

*If you want to check yours, ask your web developer to look for these three files. Happy to share what to look for.*

---

## Post 4 — The Behind-the-Build Post

**Pillar:** Behind the build  
**Hook type:** Story

---

I built SeenBy because of something that bugged me.

I kept seeing Malaysian businesses invest serious money in SEO, ads, and branding — and then I'd ask ChatGPT about their category, and their competitors would show up instead.

Not because the competitors were better.

Because they had structured their digital presence in a way that AI could actually read and cite.

It's not about gaming the system.
It's about being legible to machines that now influence human decisions.

A dentist in Bangsar shouldn't lose a patient to a competitor in Kepong just because one website has a schema markup file and the other doesn't.

That's fixable. That's what I'm working on.

---

*If you've ever Googled your own business and found something that surprised you — AI search is the same thing, but the results are much less predictable. I'd be curious what you find.*

---

## Post 5 — The Proof Post (Future: Use Client Results)

**Pillar:** Scan findings  
**Hook type:** Impressive result

*(Use this format once you have a real client result to reference. Anonymise if needed.)*

---

A client came to us with an AI visibility score of 31/100.

Their main competitor was being recommended in 3 out of 4 AI platforms for their core queries.

They weren't.

Here's what we did over 90 days:

→ Implemented llms.txt, schema markup, and updated robots.txt (took their developer 45 minutes)
→ Added a comparison page to their website covering the top 3 competitor queries
→ Addressed one AI hallucination about their location that had been spreading across platforms

90-day result: Score moved from 31 to 67.

They went from invisible in ChatGPT to being recommended for 4 of their 5 target queries.

The competitor gap closed from 3:0 to 5:3.

---

*Not every client sees results this fast. But the structural work — the files, the content, the corrections — compounds. AI models update regularly and pull from structured sources.*

*The businesses that set this up early will have a meaningful head start.*

---

## Post Ideas Bank (Unwritten — Fill In When Ready)

- "I asked AI what [local industry] competitors it knows in Malaysia — here's the full list"
- "The weirdest AI hallucination I've seen about a Malaysian brand (anonymised)"
- "Why Perplexity is different from ChatGPT for local business recommendations"
- "What happens to your AI visibility when a competitor gets a big press feature"
- "Lesson from month 1 of running SeenBy: [honest observation]"
- "The one question every Malaysian founder should ask about their AI presence"
- "I scanned [X] brands in [industry]. Here's the visibility leaderboard."

---

## Posting Notes

- **No hashtag spam** — 2–3 max, relevant ones only (#AISearch #DigitalMarketing #MalaysiaBusiness)
- **No link in the post body** — if linking, put it in the first comment
- **Respond to every comment within 2 hours** — this is how LinkedIn distributes further
- **Tag sparingly** — only tag people you're genuinely referencing, not for reach
- **Best times (Malaysia):** Tuesday–Thursday, 8–9am or 12–1pm
